title: pelargonium × coeleste
intro: Pelargonium × coeleste, also known as the Mountain Rose, is a hybrid plant belonging to the Geraniaceae family, derived from the crossing of Pelargonium fasciculatum and Pelargonium capitatum. The medicinal properties of Pelargonium × coeleste are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, particularly rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which are known for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, Pelargonium × coeleste is often used as a tea, infused in hot water to release its medicinal properties, or as a tincture, extracted using a solvent to concentrate its active compounds.
